Sale.title: Sale Record
Sale.description: A record of a single completed transaction on the platform.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the sale occurred.

buyerId.description: The UID of the user who made the purchase.

sellerId.description: The UID of the user who sold the prompt (if applicable).

promptIds.description: An array of prompt IDs included in the sale.

gross.description: The total transaction amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g., cents).

platformFee.description: The portion of the gross amount taken by the platform, in cents.

sellerEarning.description: The net amount earned by the seller, in cents.

UserProfile.title: User Profile
UserProfile.description: Stores public and private profile information for a user.

uid.description: The unique identifier for the user, matching the Firebase Auth UID.

username.description: The user's unique, public-facing username for URLs.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

role.description: The user's role in the application.

purchasedPrompts.description: An array of prompt IDs that the user has purchased.

credits.description: Credit balance purchased by the user.

earnings.description: Credit balance earned from prompt sales, available for payout.

payoutStatus.description: Current status of a pending payout request.

PublicProfile.title: Public User Profile
PublicProfile.description: Stores only the public-facing information for a user.

Prompt.title: Prompt
Prompt.description: Represents a single AI prompt listing in the marketplace.

isPrivate.description: If true, this prompt is only visible to users with a PRO subscription.

Model.title: Model
Model.description: Represents an AI model used to generate prompts.

PromptPrivateContent.title: Prompt Private Content
PromptPrivateContent.description: The actual, protected content of the prompt.

Cart.title: Cart
Cart.description: Represents a shopping cart for a user, containing a list of prompts they intend to purchase.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this cart. (Relationship: User 1:1 Cart)

promptIds.description: References to Prompts in the cart. (Relationship: Cart 1:N Prompt)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cart was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a completed order placed by a user.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

promptIds.description: References to Prompts included in the order. (Relationship: Order 1:N Prompt)

orderDate.description: Date and time when the order was placed.

totalAmount.description: Total amount paid for the order.

paymentMethod.description: Payment method used for the order (e.g., credit card, PayPal).

status.description: Status of the order (e.g., pending, processing, shipped, delivered).

PayoutRequest.title: Payout Request
PayoutRequest.description: Represents a user's request to withdraw their earnings.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a notification for a user (e.g., a sale, a payout status change).

definition.description: Stores user profile information, including roles and purchases.
Stores shopping carts for each user. Path-based ownership ensures clear access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who owns the cart.

definition.description: Stores order data for each user. Path-based ownership ensures that users can only access their own order history.

params.name: userId
params.description: The ID of the user who placed the order.

definition.description: Publicly accessible data for a prompt listing.
Publicly accessible list of AI models.
Private content of a prompt, accessible only after purchase or by admins.
Stores all user requests for payouts. Accessible only by admins.
Centralized collection for all sale transactions for analytics and admin purposes.

params.name: saleId
params.description: The ID of the sale transaction.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support a shopping cart and order management system for the Promptly application. It focuses on user-centric data organization and authorization independence.  The `/users/{userId}/carts` collection stores individual user carts, linking each cart directly to a user. This path-based ownership ensures clear ownership and straightforward security rules. The `/users/{userId}/orders` collection similarly stores user-specific order data. Both cart and order data are segregated into user-specific subcollections to maintain a homogeneous security posture. This design avoids complex queries and simplifies security rules, as access control is primarily based on the authenticated user's ID. The absence of a global 'carts' or 'orders' collection enforces that users can only access their own carts and orders. This structure facilitates secure `list` operations because each user's data is isolated within their own subcollection. No denormalization is explicitly required in this basic cart/order structure as ownership is directly implied by the path.
